name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is the largest U.S. payday lender, offering same-day payday loans, installment loans, title loans, and lines of credit across 27 states.
description_long:
Founded in July 1997 by George Dean Johnson Jr. and Billy Webster in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America grew into the largest payday loan company in the United States. Acquired by Mexican conglomerate Grupo Elektra in 2012 for $10.50 per share, the company remains headquartered in downtown Spartanburg in a roughly 50,000-square-foot building. It is a founding member of the Community Financial Services Association of America (CFSA), the payday lending industry's primary self-regulatory body, and holds state licenses in every state where it operates. With 800–889 retail locations and a functioning online platform, it primarily serves consumers who need fast access to small-dollar credit and cannot qualify for traditional bank financing.

Advance America's core product is the payday loan — a short-term advance against the borrower's next paycheck, with amounts and fees strictly governed by state law. California borrowers can access up to $255 at a maximum 15% fee on up to a 31-day term; Florida allows up to $500 at a 10% fee plus a $5 verification fee on a 7–31 day term; Ohio law restricts the company to installment loans only, capped at $1,000 over up to 12 months at a regulated 28% APR. Beyond payday loans, the company offers installment loans with multi-month repayment schedules, auto title loans secured by the borrower's vehicle, and revolving lines of credit up to approximately $4,000 — all available in select states. In-store funding is typically disbursed in roughly 30 minutes with a valid ID and proof of income. Online applicants approved before 10:30 AM ET can receive same-day ACH deposits by 5 PM ET. No hard credit pull is required for most products, making approval accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history.

Advance America's primary differentiators are scale and speed. Its 800-plus retail locations across 27 states make it among the most physically accessible short-term lenders in the country, and its in-store same-day funding model is a genuine advantage for consumers in acute financial emergencies. The online portal at web.advanceamerica.net supports full account management, loan applications, repayment tracking, and multi-factor authentication. As a CFSA founding member, Advance America participates in the industry's extended payment plan program — allowing borrowers facing hardship to extend repayment without additional fees, a commitment not universal among payday lenders.

The company's accessibility for credit-impaired borrowers comes with a significant tradeoff. Payday loan APRs typically range from approximately 350% to 700% depending on the state, placing these among the most expensive forms of consumer credit available. The California cap of $255 limits utility for emergencies requiring more than that amount. Product availability varies substantially by state, meaning some consumers may find only installment loans or no Advance America presence in their area. For borrowers who can repay quickly and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives, Advance America delivers reliably on its promise of fast, accessible cash. For those at risk of repeat borrowing or rollover, the costs compound rapidly and can worsen an already precarious financial situation.
address: 2600 Oswell St spc c, Bakersfield, CA 93306

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee policy found. Advance America is a loan provider — fees are incurred upon disbursement and are non-refundable.

tiers.name: Payday Loan
features: Typical fee: ~$15 per $100 borrowed (state-dependent)
APR range: approximately 350%–700% depending on state
California: max $255 loan, max 15% fee, up to 31-day term
Florida: max $500, 10% fee + $5 verification fee, 7–31 day term
No hard credit pull required for most products
Same-day in-store funding in approximately 30 minutes
Available in-store and online in select states

tiers.name: Installment Loan
features: Multi-month repayment structure
Ohio: max $1,000, up to 12 months, capped at 28% APR by state law
Some state APRs exceed 200%
Available in select states only
No hard credit pull required for most products
Fixed scheduled payments over loan term

tiers.name: Title Loan
features: Secured by borrower's vehicle title
Available in select states only
Vehicle must be owned outright or carry sufficient equity
Potentially larger amounts than unsecured payday loans
Same-day in-store funding available

tiers.name: Line of Credit
features: Revolving credit product — draw funds as needed
Up to approximately $4,000 credit limit in select states
Reusable as balance is repaid
Available in select states only
Managed via online account portal

pros: Largest payday loan company in the U.S. with 800–889 retail locations across 27 states
Same-day in-store cash disbursement in approximately 30 minutes with ID and proof of income
No hard credit pull required for most products — accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history
Online same-day ACH deposit available for approvals submitted before 10:30 AM ET
Founded in 1997 with nearly 30 years of operation in consumer short-term lending
BBB-accredited business and CFSA founding member with extended payment plan commitments for hardship cases
Multiple product types available: payday loans, installment loans, title loans, and revolving lines of credit up to ~$4,000
cons: Payday loan APRs range from approximately 350% to 700% — among the most expensive consumer credit products available
California borrowers are capped at a $255 maximum loan amount, limiting usefulness for larger emergencies
Product availability and loan limits vary significantly by state; some states offer installment loans only
No money-back guarantee or post-disbursement cancellation period identified
Owned by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican conglomerate — not a community lender, credit union, or mission-driven nonprofit
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit who need emergency cash within hours and have no lower-cost alternatives
Unbanked or underbanked adults who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Borrowers needing small amounts ($100–$500) to bridge a short-term gap before their next paycheck
Consumers in one of 27 licensed states who have access to a nearby Advance America retail branch or its online platform
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for credit-impaired consumers facing a genuine short-term cash emergency — its no-hard-credit-pull approval and 30-minute in-store funding are real and accessible for people other lenders turn away. The critical caveat is cost: payday loan APRs of 350%–700% make these among the most expensive loans available, and repeat borrowing or rollover can accelerate financial distress rather than relieve it. This is a last-resort, short-duration tool — not a path toward financial stability.
services: Payday loans (in-store and online, amounts capped by state law)
Revolving lines of credit (up to ~$4,000, select states)
Online loan application via web portal
Account management and loan status tracking (web.advanceamerica.net)
Same-day ACH direct deposit for online approvals before 10:30 AM ET
Extended payment plans for borrowers facing financial hardship (CFSA program)
Customer support via phone (1-877-505-0701) and email (comments@advanceamerica.net)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
